title: Identifying benefits
description: Identify key terms related to benefits, including: health insurance contributions, retirement benefits, sick leave, vacation pay, flexible spending account, health savings account, workers compensation, life insurance, and disability insurance open enrollment, qualifying events.

assessment_items.question_content:
Imagine getting a new job that offers to pay a portion of a $\$600$ monthly health insurance premium. 

**Which benefit is this an example of?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You're saving part of your salary in a special account called 401(k), that your company provides. When you retire, you can use this money. 

**What benefit is this?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You're feeling unwell and decide to stay home from work for a few days. Your job continues to pay you as if you're working.

**What benefit is this?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You're planning a vacation, and your job allows you to go but continues to pay you. 

**What benefit is this?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You save money in a special account for covering medical costs. If you don't use it all this year, it rolls over to the next year. 

**What benefit does this describe?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You slipped at work, hurt your leg and needed medical treatment. Your job benefits cover this hospital cost.

**What benefit does this describe?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Your job offers a benefit that pays an amount of money to your family if you pass away.

**What benefit is this an example of?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

An accident prevents you from working for an extended time. During this time, your job benefits provide you with some of your usual income.

**What benefit is this describing?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Before taxes, you set aside part of your paycheck. You use this money to cover medical costs for the year.

**What benefit is this?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

There's a special time of the year when all employees can select or change their job benefits.

**What is this called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You recently got married, and although it's not the usual time, your job lets you change your benefits.

**What is this called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

If you are sick or simply taking some time off, you still receive a paycheck with this benefit.

**What is this benefit called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
